verdict: Is the parameter latent? NO -- it is SURFACE-READABLE, not model-computed. The op-TYPE is model-latent (depth-2 probe 0.413 > external-I/O 0.272), but the PARAMETER given type is decoded as well by a trivial I/O classifier as by the model residual (probe 0.493 vs surface 0.529, chance 0.303). DEPLOYABILITY on param-first-op tasks: the param IS the deployable bottleneck (oracle_full 0.095 >> oracle_type 0.007), but the model probe barely delivers (probe_full 0.014) and the CHEAP SURFACE pipeline delivers as well or better (surface_full 0.027) -- you do not need the model for the param. wrong_param 0.0 (content-causal). Sharp localization of C30: the forward pass COMPUTES the op-type (latent, elicitable) but only READS the parameter off surface I/O (no privileged model knowledge to elicit).
